quote.text: The demo went viral months ago. The headline was Tuesday. I started shipping the box on Wednesday.

narrative.latestInsight: Most people use AI daily but only for grammar and basics — they feel under-leveraged, not behind. CurXor closes the gap from chatbot to always-on scale.

insightBacklog.quote: I use it almost every day but I feel like I'm not using it for what I could be — just grammar and basic stuff.

bios.short: Founder @ CurXor — sovereign AI desk on metal you own. Ten Claws · local inference · $3,999 once · $0/mo API.
bios.medium: Founder & CEO, CurXor. Building the sovereign AI appliance — ten Claws on bare metal, Flight Command, $0/mo inference. Jun 18 named it in public; Jun 19 started shipping.
bios.linkedinHeadline: Founder & CEO at CurXor · Sovereign AI desk on metal you own · Ten Claws · local inference · $3,999 once · $0/mo API · Building in public
bios.heroTagline: Your AI team. On a box you own.
bios.oneLiner: CurXor — a desk appliance that runs ten AI agents on hardware you own. $3,999 once. No API rent.
bios.commentReplyPlain: Plain version: a $3,999 AI box on your desk with CurXor OS — ten agents that run locally 24/7. No monthly API bill. "Orchestrator" is the vision; the box is what you buy.

threeActs.name: Prove
threeActs.os: CurXor OS on Ubuntu 24.04 — four pillars

threeActs.name: Sovereign
threeActs.os: Fully sovereign CurXor OS — not Linux-dependent

thesisAccents: The next interface isn't a device. It's the orchestrator on your desk.
Not another device. The desk that runs your world.
dreamState.investorParagraph: CurXor is a sovereign AI system — not a chat app, not a cloud subscription. Custom desk hardware runs CurXor OS, a full vertical stack built for local inference, autonomous agents, and operator control from the metal up. Ten Claws are your digital employees — capital, creation, outreach, household, devices — minted in The Forge and coordinated through Flight Command and the Claw Cafe. Outbound action crosses a physical egress boundary you control; cognition stays local by default. Today we prove the stack on MS-S1-class metal. After investment, we ship CurXor-designed hardware. The long-term destination is a fully sovereign OS — no Linux dependency, no rented intelligence — so your AI team isn't a feature on someone else's platform. It's yours, from silicon to Claw.

tweets.product:
What is CurXor?

A box on your desk that runs ten AI agents 24/7 — on your metal, not in someone else's cloud.

$3,999 once · $0/mo API rent.

The orchestrator on your desk is what we call it once you see the whole picture.
tweets.productShort:
CurXor = hardware + OS for autonomous AI on your desk.

Plug in. Ten agents work locally. You own the box. No API subscription.

curxor.ai
tweets.founder:
Someone asked what "the orchestrator on your desk" means.

Plain English: it's a $3,999 AI computer that ships with CurXor OS — ten agents that run on your metal 24/7, not a ChatGPT bill.

The fancy line is the vision. The box is the product.
tweets.founderShort:
Fair question. Simple version:

AI hardware box + CurXor OS. Ten autonomous agents on your desk. Local inference. You own it.

"Orchestrator" = it coordinates your agents and (eventually) your devices — not another chatbot tab.

originStory.title: The viral moment
originStory.summary: Months after OpenClaw went viral, SpaceX went public and Cursor's acquisition option made sovereign AI impossible to ignore. Ankur posted the CurXor vision publicly, rebranded to CurXor HQ, and started building within 24 hours.
originStory.convictionOrigin: OpenClaw viral moment — product should ship out of the box
publicCommitment: Rebrand to CurXor HQ — name, bio, header image
originStory.visionStatement: A sovereign edge box serving as your personal server and codebase. A centralized cognitive hub orchestrating a localized AI ecosystem.

mission: Make autonomous AI ownable — not rented, not hosted, not locked inside someone else's cloud. Ship the full stack working out of the box on hardware you control.

timeline.label: Early 2026

Software scaffold complete; hardware validation pending
